title: Planetary Poker

{{Quote|Forget that silly game, Chewie.|Han Solo|<ref name="Rebellious Robot" />}}
'''Planetary Poker''' was a card game that [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] were known to play on long [[Realspace/Legends|space]] voyages. The game consisted of a deck of cards, a hand for each player, and [[Gold/Legends|gold]] chips to keep track of variables in the game. At least two people could play the game.<ref name="Rebellious Robot">''[[The Mystery of the Rebellious Robot]]''</ref>

Sometime after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]],<ref name="Date">''[[The Mystery of the Rebellious Robot]]'' is set following the events of {{Film|IV}}, which include the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].</ref> the [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], the [[Pilot/Legends|co-pilot]] of the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|light freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], played Planetary Poker aboard the [[Light freighter/Legends|ship]] during a voyage to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] with supplies for their friend [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] [[super-vaporator]] project. During the game, R2-D2's [[Computer monitor/Legends|computer monitor]] became inoperational, forcing [[Gender/Legends|him]] to cease playing. Chewbacca became upset, as he was winning at the [[Time/Legends|time]], but the [[Freighter/Legends|vessel's]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], told him to forget the "silly" game and to [[Oil/Legends|oil]] R2-D2 to restore his ability to function.<ref name="Rebellious Robot" />

==Behind the scenes==
Planetary Poker appeared in the children's book ''[[The Mystery of the Rebellious Robot]]'', which was published in [[1979]].<ref name="Rebellious Robot" /> The book was authored by [[Eleanor Ehrhardt]]<ref name="Starwars Blog">{{SW|url=news/6-vintage-star-wars-picture-books|text=6 Vintage ''Star Wars'' Picture Books}}</ref> and illustrated by [[Mark Corcoran]].<ref name="Rebellious Robot" /> [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and R2-D2 play the game in "[[Light Side Dark Side]]," a [[Canon|non-canon]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic strip]] in ''[[Star Wars Gamer Special Edition]]'', which was released in [[2000]]. The story was written by [[Aaron Williams]].<ref name="LSDS">''[[Star Wars Gamer Special Edition]]''</ref>
